key_insights.insight: It's important not to let a job define your identity; you will become whoever you go on to be.
key_insights.quote: You will be whoever you go on to be. That's not going to change. You are still there. You're letting this thing influence all these views about yourself.

key_insights.insight: Teams are organic and ever-changing, so your role within a team is crucial to its success.
key_insights.quote: Teams are like these organic ever-changing you know you could bring one person in and it have a massive impact on the team you take one person out it can ruin a team.

key_insights.insight: Being honest is crucial, but that doesn't give you a license to be a dickhead; compassion is key.
key_insights.quote: It's really crucial to be honest. It's really crucial to be authentic, but that doesn't mean you get a license to be a dickhead.

key_insights.insight: It's better to honestly state when someone is not a fit for an organization rather than let them continue to fail.
key_insights.quote: It's better to be honest with them and say you know what this isn't for you then to kind of allow them to keep failing I think that can be very cruel to people.

key_insights.insight: When giving feedback, be compassionate to get a better response.
key_insights.quote: Be compassionate, be sensitive to the fact that you're going to get a better response from someone if you're just nice to them.

key_insights.insight: You have to know when to remove yourself from a situation if your emotions are too high.
key_insights.quote: There's nothing wrong with having a chimp, but you have to know when to get out of the room if that's what's going on. Don't bring your emotion into an environment where you're expecting people to perform at their very best.

key_insights.insight: It's crucial to fully commit and support decisions made by leadership, even if you disagree privately.
key_insights.quote: If you sit in a meeting room and you agree with something and you say yeah okay whilst i don't agree with it i buy in you know what i mean i've given you my point of view you've said it's not what the way we're going to go but i buy in and then you walk out the room and you're like i don't fucking buy that that is that is one of the worst losing behaviors you can have.

actionable_advice.advice: Resist assigning yourself labels based on your job or current role.
actionable_advice.how_to: Actively explore new skills and challenges outside of your comfort zone to avoid stagnation and maintain a sense of self independent of your profession.

actionable_advice.advice: When joining a new organization, prioritize listening and understanding before making changes.
actionable_advice.how_to: Conduct one-on-one meetings with team members, solicit advice from mentors, and avoid making rash decisions in the first 100 days.

memorable_stories: Fran's brother, David Miller's, experience with doping scandal in professional cycling
Fran's experience of going through brain scan with concerns of tumors
Fran's account of setting up her agency with a friend and their contrasting 10-year plans.
contrarian_claims: It's not possible to have it all; choices must be made, and compromise is sometimes necessary.
Quitting is an underrated skill; it often precedes a new beginning or a more successful venture.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - How To Chase Your Dreams Without Fear Holding You Back with Fran Millar
